title: espeletia × meridensis
intro: Espeletia × meridensis, also known as the Andean tree or tree espeletia, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that grows in the Andes mountains. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Its leaves and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and its extracts have also been used in herbal remedies, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine.
